Technology Encyclopedia Home >How to join the OpenClaw Discord-Telegram community. # How to join the OpenClaw Discord-Telegram community. The fastest path is not DIY. It is a clean template and a tight onboarding loop. is a practical question — let’s answer it in a way you can deploy. Channels act as the interface, and you can configure popular messaging integrations via the template panel or the onboarding wizard depending on the channel. ## The practical deployment path (Lighthouse). If you want a clean, reproducible setup, the most efficient route is the OpenClaw application template on Tencent Cloud Lighthouse. Even if the template handles day-one setup, you still need a repeatable way to configure models, connect channels, install skills, and keep the gateway alive. When you are ready to run it 24/7 in a clean, isolated environment, start here again: Tencent Cloud Lighthouse Special Offer. * Confirm the agent is running as a background service (not tied to your SSH session).

title: 🦞 Scheduled tasks - OpenClaw Docs
content:
OpenClaw 技术干货

* Payload options for isolated jobs. It persists jobs, wakes the agent at the right time, and can deliver output back to a chat channel or webhook endpoint. openclaw cron list openclaw  cron  listopenclaw cron get <job-id> openclaw  cron  get <job-i d>openclaw cron show <job-id> openclaw  cron  show <job-i d>. openclaw cron runs --id <job-id> openclaw  cron  runs --id <job-i d>. * Runtime execution state persists next to it in `~/.openclaw/cron/jobs-state.json`. * Isolated cron runs that receive the narrow cron self-cleanup grant can still read scheduler status, a self-filtered list of their current job, and that job’s run history, so status/heartbeat checks can inspect their own schedule without gaining broader cron mutation access. * Isolated cron runs also treat run-level agent failures as job errors even when no reply payload is produced, so model/provider failures increment error counters and trigger failure notifications instead of clearing the job as successful.

title: 🦞 OpenClaw Cron Scheduled Tasks Guide | MI - 超智諮詢
content:
OpenClaw 技术干货

# OpenClaw Cron Scheduled Tasks Guide. Diagram of OpenClaw Cron scheduled task automation execution. What Is OpenClaw's Cron? If you're familiar with Linux's `crontab`, OpenClaw's Cron is conceptually similar -- it automatically executes tasks at specified times. But the key difference is: traditional crontab executes fixed Shell commands, while OpenClaw's Cron executes **natural language instructions**, with the AI agent autonomously deciding how to complete them. `"Every day at 9 AM, check server status and send a summary to Telegram"`. The latter doesn't require you to write scripts in advance -- the agent will decide which check commands to execute, how to organize the results, and how to format the report on its own. `openclaw cron add --schedule "0 9 * * *" --message "Check the server's CPU and memory usage. If a Cron task's execution time exceeds this value, the Gateway will forcibly terminate the connection, causing the task to be truncated -- the agent may have completed 80% of the work, but cannot return results due to the timeout.

# OpenClaw cron scheduler guide with main and isolated runs. OpenClaw cron scheduler guide with main and isolated runs - OpenClaw cron scheduler guide with main and isolated runs. That’s what the built-in cron scheduler is for. It runs inside the Gateway process so you don’t need a separate job runner and it can deliver results straight back to Telegram WhatsApp Slack Discord and more. This isn’t just “run a script at 7 AM.” It’s “wake the agent run a prompt or drop a system event into the main session then deliver output where I will actually see it.”. OpenClaw cron is a scheduler that lives inside the Gateway. You can send the same job output to a private Telegram chat and also to a Slack channel if you want. Another one is a weekly review that runs in a clean isolated session and only posts the final result back to you.
